name: Ciskei (1981–1994)

colors.symbolism: the bend sinister and the edging of the crane

description: A sky-blue field crossed by a white bend sinister (a diagonal white band running from the lower hoist to the upper fly), with a Blue Crane (Indwe) in black silhouette, edged white, over the centre.
history: Flag of Ciskei, one of the four nominally independent 'Bantustans' created by apartheid-era South Africa as a 'homeland' for the Xhosa-speaking people of the Ciskei region. The Ciskei Flag Act specified a blue field with a white bend sinister and, over all, a Blue Crane proper edged white. Independence declared 4 December 1981, internationally unrecognised; reincorporated into South Africa in 1994.
